intro:
There is no information available about the plant Rubus menkei microgene multifidus. However, I can provide information about Rubus species, which are known as raspberries or brambles. If the plant in question is a Rubus species, here is a paragraph:

Rubus species are a group of thorny shrubs in the rose family, characterized by their aggregate fruits and biennial or perennial habit. The Rubus species have been used in traditional medicine for their potential health benefits, including their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease, cancer, and neurodegenerative disorders. The bioactive constituents of Rubus species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and ellagic acid, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Rubus species are commonly prepared as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are used in herbal medicine to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and promote antioxidant defenses.
